library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

identity.name: SDL_SetAudioPostmixCallback
identity.titleArabic:
Set a callback that fires when data is about to be fed to an audio
device

summary.actualTranslationArabic:
Set a callback that fires when data is about to be fed to an audio
device
summary.actualMeaningArabic: تمثل هذه الدالة عملية تشغيل SDL Set الصوت Postmix Callback داخل SDL3، وتنفذ فعلاً عند استدعائها لا مجرد تعريف نظري.
summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى تشغيل SDL Set الصوت Postmix Callback داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic: القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه يعيد true عند النجاح وfalse عند الفشل؛ استخدم SDL_GetError لمعرفة السبب. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription:
Set a callback that fires when data is about to be fed to an audio
device.

parameters.name: devid
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل devid من النوع SDL_AudioDeviceID القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: the ID of an opened audio device.

parameters.name: callback
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل callback من النوع SDL_AudioPostmixCallback القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: a callback function to be called. Can be NULL.

parameters.name: userdata
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل userdata من النوع void * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.
parameters.originalDescription: app-controlled pointer passed to callback. Can be NULL.

remarks:
This is useful for accessing the final mix, perhaps for writing a
visualizer or applying a final effect to the audio data before
playback.

The buffer is the final mix of all bound audio streams on an opened
device; this callback will fire regularly for any device that is both
opened and unpaused. If there is no new data to mix, either because no
streams are bound to the device or all the streams are empty, this
callback will still fire with the entire buffer set to silence.

This callback is allowed to make changes to the data; the contents of
the buffer after this call is what is ultimately passed along to the
hardware.

The callback is always provided the data in float format (values from
-1.0f to 1.0f), but the number of channels or sample rate may be
different than the format the app requested when opening the device; SDL
might have had to manage a conversion behind the scenes, or the playback
might have jumped to new physical hardware when a system default
changed, etc. These details may change between calls. Accordingly, the
size of the buffer might change between calls as well.

This callback can run at any time, and from any thread; if you need
to serialize access to your app's data, you should provide and use a
mutex or other synchronization device.

All of this to say: there are specific needs this callback can
fulfill, but it is not the simplest interface. Apps should generally
provide audio in their preferred format through an SDL_AudioStream and let SDL handle the
difference.

This function is extremely time-sensitive; the callback should do the
least amount of work possible and return as quickly as it can. The
longer the callback runs, the higher the risk of audio dropouts or other
problems.

This function will block until the audio device is in between
iterations, so any existing callback that might be running will finish
before this function sets the new callback and returns.

Setting a NULL callback function disables any previously-set
callback.
details.returns: يعيد true عند النجاح وfalse عند الفشل؛ استخدم SDL_GetError لمعرفة السبب.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
